description_long: This is a stylized, vintage biplane with a predominantly light green fuselage and cream-colored wings, featuring roundels reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The wings are stacked in a biplane configuration, supported by multiple struts, and display bold circular insignias in blue, white, and red. The nose houses a large, exposed radial engine with a wooden propeller, and the undercarriage consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The tail section is painted with a tricolor rudder and a horizontal stabilizer, while the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windscreen and a visible pilot figure. The overall design is compact and cartoonish, with exaggerated proportions and simplified details. The surface finish appears matte, with visible panel lines and painted markings.
description: A stylized, vintage biplane with green and cream coloring, roundel insignias, an exposed radial engine, and an open cockpit. The design is compact, cartoonish, and features a tricolor tail and wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing the tricolor tail, open cockpit, and stacked biplane wings with roundels.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the dual wings, roundel insignias, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_2: Rear side view emphasizing the tail section, wing struts, and the exposed engine at the front.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the large radial engine, wooden propeller, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, high-mounted wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted exposed radial piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; rudder painted in tricolor.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage, with a small windscreen.
